title: `strict` mode is undercut by disabling `noImplicitAny`
reasoning: This config advertises strict TypeScript checking, but `noImplicitAny: false` explicitly turns off one of the most important strictness checks. That allows new implicit-`any` paths to compile, which can hide type regressions and runtime errors in code that otherwise appears to be under strict checking.
recommendation: Remove the override and rely on `strict: true`, or set `noImplicitAny: true` explicitly if the intent is full strictness.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are provided for compiler-option behavior, and a passing typecheck here would still miss implicit-`any` because the option is disabled in the config itself.
suggestedRegressionTest: Run `npm run typecheck` against a file that intentionally introduces an implicit `any` and assert the compiler reports an error once the config is fixed.
minimumFixScope: Update `tsconfig.json` only; no source changes are required.
